name: substack-ops
description: Substack CLI + 26-tool MCP server. Your IDE drafts replies via propose_reply. No AI API keys.
long_description: Substack-OPS is a standalone Substack CLI and 26-tool MCP server. Your IDE (Claude Desktop / Cursor) drafts the replies — there are zero AI API keys, no paid LLM dependencies, and no hidden services. The MCP server exposes propose_reply / confirm_reply triage workflow plus inbox, post, draft, and reply tooling that talks directly to Substack's web endpoints. Authenticate once with `substack-ops auth login --browser chrome` to grab the session cookie automatically, or set SUBSTACK_PUBLICATION_URL / SUBSTACK_USER_ID / SUBSTACK_SESSION_TOKEN. Distributed via PyPI (`uvx substack-ops`), the official MCP Registry, and Glama.

publication_url.title: Publication URL
publication_url.description: Your Substack publication URL (e.g. https://you.substack.com/). Optional if you've already run `substack-ops auth login`.

user_id.title: User ID
user_id.description: Your Substack numeric user id. Optional if your mcp.json already has it under mcpServers.substack-api.env.

session_token.title: Session Token
session_token.description: Substack session cookie value (the s%3A... string). Easier path: run `substack-ops auth login --browser chrome` and the cookie is auto-grabbed.
